warnings: Item 20 transfer tables appear to cut off mid-table (only through Michigan shown); full state-by-state data may be incomplete.

extracted.brandBackground: Dunkin' is a QSR brand founded in 1954 offering donuts, coffee, espresso, bagels, muffins, croissants, breakfast sandwiches, and other food and beverage products; franchising has been offered since 1955 and the system had 9,999 U.S. restaurants as of December 28, 2025.
extracted.conceptRationale: Dunkin' is a quick-service restaurant chain focused on coffee, donuts, and breakfast items sold through counter and drive-thru service.
extracted.staffingRationale: Item 15 requires at least two trained individuals to manage the restaurant at all times, and Item 11 describes a full crew-based operation with dedicated employees.

leadership.name: Paul Brown
leadership.role: Board of Managers Member / Inspire Brands CEO
leadership.background: CEO of Inspire Brands since its formation in February 2018 and Board Member of Dunkin' Donuts Franchising LLC since December 2020.
leadership.whyItMatters: Top executive overseeing the entire Inspire Brands portfolio including Dunkin'.

leadership.name: Katherine Jaspon
leadership.role: Chief Financial Officer / Assistant Secretary / Board of Managers Member
leadership.background: CFO of Dunkin' Donuts Franchising LLC since April 2017 and Inspire Brands CFO since July 2021.
leadership.whyItMatters: Controls financial strategy and reporting for the franchisor entity.

leadership.name: Scott Catlett
leadership.role: Chief Legal & Administrative Officer / Secretary / Board of Managers Member
leadership.background: Joined Inspire Brands and Dunkin' in September 2025; previously Chief Legal and Franchise Officer at Yum Brands.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees all legal, franchise compliance, and administrative matters.

leadership.name: Scott Murphy
leadership.background: Chief Brand Officer of Inspire Brands and Dunkin' since November 2023; previously President of Dunkin' from December 2020 to November 2023.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads brand strategy, marketing, and menu innovation for Dunkin'.

leadership.name: Ricardo Colon
leadership.role: Senior Vice President / Chief Operating Officer – Dunkin'
leadership.background: SVP and COO of Dunkin' since January 2021; previously SVP National Operations and Development from December 2017.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directly responsible for day-to-day U.S. restaurant operations and franchisee support.

leadership.name: Jason Maceda
leadership.background: Inspire Brands Chief Portfolio Officer since December 2025; previously Chief Development Officer from January 2024.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads franchise development and new unit growth strategy.

cohorts.label: All 2025 Franchised Restaurants – 1st Quartile
cohorts.basis: Average AUV (Gross Sales) for highest-performing quartile of 7,010 franchised Dunkin' restaurants operating full year in 2025, excluding new openings, extended closures, Multi-Brand, self-serve, and part-time units.

cohorts.label: All 2025 Franchised Restaurants – 2nd Quartile
cohorts.basis: Average AUV for second quartile of 2025 Franchised Restaurants.

cohorts.label: All 2025 Franchised Restaurants – 3rd Quartile
cohorts.basis: Average AUV for third quartile of 2025 Franchised Restaurants.

cohorts.label: All 2025 Franchised Restaurants – 4th Quartile
cohorts.basis: Average AUV for lowest-performing quartile of 2025 Franchised Restaurants.

cohorts.label: All 2025 Franchised Restaurants – Overall Average
cohorts.basis: Overall average AUV across all 7,010 qualifying franchised Dunkin' restaurants in 2025.

cohorts.label: Traditional/Freestanding Pad or Building
cohorts.basis: Average AUV for freestanding pad or building traditional locations in 2025.

cohorts.label: Traditional/Other
cohorts.basis: Average AUV for other traditional (shopping center/storefront) locations in 2025.

cohorts.label: Non-Traditional/SDO – Gas or C-store
cohorts.basis: Average AUV for gas station or convenience store SDO locations in 2025.

cohorts.label: Non-Traditional/SDO – Airport
cohorts.basis: Average AUV for airport SDO locations in 2025.

cohorts.label: Non-Traditional/SDO – Other
cohorts.basis: Average AUV for other non-traditional SDO locations (casinos, hospitals, colleges, etc.) in 2025.

cohorts.label: Traditional Drive-Thru Restaurants
cohorts.basis: Average AUV for traditional 2025 Franchised Restaurants with a drive-thru window.

cohorts.label: Traditional No Drive-Thru Restaurants
cohorts.basis: Average AUV for traditional 2025 Franchised Restaurants without a drive-thru window.

item19.notes: Item 19 discloses gross sales (AUV) only; no EBITDA, net income, or profitability table is provided. AUVs are unaudited self-reported figures from franchisee POS systems. Excluded: 314 new openings, 1,261 extended closures, 36 Multi-Brand, 111 self-serve, 25 part-time/seasonal, 81 closed units, all company-owned, and all 1,219 Combo Restaurants.

lineItems.notes: Varies by Development Area Type (Type 1=$90k, Type 6=$40k); lump sum due upon signing Franchise Agreement.
Freestanding restaurant; cost to construct or retrofit building, excluding land purchase.
Freestanding restaurant; land development, landscaping, electrical/water hookup, paving, lighting.
Freestanding restaurant; architectural, engineering, and legal fees.
Freestanding restaurant; furnishings, installations, equipment, trade fixtures, smallwares; includes 10% tax/delivery estimate.
Freestanding restaurant; POS systems (2-4 terminals), BOH software, digital signage, drive-thru equipment, and related technology.
Freestanding restaurant; excludes government-imposed impact fees which can be $87,000+ in some markets.
Variable; depends on location, lease terms, and structure; affiliates use net-net-net leases.
Freestanding restaurant; initial inventory of food ingredients, paper goods, cleaning supplies.
Pre-opening payroll, utility deposits, petty cash, distribution center fees, miscellaneous expenses.
Freestanding restaurant; lump sum or down payment before opening; annual renewal is ongoing.
Travel, lodging, meals, uniforms for 2 attendees of 4-8 week Dunkin' Training Program; wages not included.
Freestanding restaurant; minimum $10,000 for standard restaurant; $5,000 for SDO.

lineItems.category: Additional Funds for First 3 Months of Operation
lineItems.notes: Freestanding restaurant; working capital estimate for first 3 months; may be zero if sales cover expenses.

flatMonthlyFees.name: POS System and BOH Technical Maintenance

hiddenCosts.name: NDCP Membership Fee
hiddenCosts.description: One-time fee to join National DCP LLC, the mandatory purchasing and distribution cooperative.

hiddenCosts.name: Supplier Sample Testing Reimbursement
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee must reimburse franchisor $1,000–$10,000 to test samples from any proposed unapproved supplier.

hiddenCosts.name: Government Impact Fees
hiddenCosts.description: Local government utility connection or impact fees estimated at $87,000 or more in some markets; excluded from Item 7 ranges.

hiddenCosts.name: Delivery Vehicle
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisees may be required to purchase a delivery vehicle meeting franchisor standards; cost not included in Item 7 totals.

hiddenCosts.name: Remodel / Refurbishment Costs
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee bears full cost of required refurbishments and remodels at dates specified in the Franchise Agreement.

hiddenCosts.name: Payment Services Fee (Inspire Payment Services)
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisor may derive 0%–6% of invoiced amounts as a service fee for billing and administrative services on vendor payments.

hiddenCosts.name: Credit Card Processing / Payment Terminal Fees
hiddenCosts.description: Monthly bank and service fees plus fixed and variable transactional fees for approved credit card program; hardware $500–$700/unit.

hiddenCosts.name: Stored Value Card Transaction Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Fee of $0–$0.02 per in-store SVC activation, reload, or purchase transaction.

hiddenCosts.name: Fraud Prevention Services
hiddenCosts.description: Fee of $0–$0.02 per online/mobile/remote ordering transaction for required fraud prevention services.

hiddenCosts.name: Training Cancellation Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $1,000/person if cancelled 8–14 days before class; $2,000/person if cancelled 7 or fewer days before class.

hiddenCosts.name: Late Fee and Interest
hiddenCosts.description: 1.5% per month (or highest rate permitted by law) on overdue amounts.

hiddenCosts.name: Transfer Fee
hiddenCosts.description: For Dunkin' restaurants operated 3+ years: $12,500 base plus $5,000–$20,000 based on trailing 12-month gross sales; plus $2,000 fixed documentation fee per transferee.

hiddenCosts.name: Renewal Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Amount varies; due upon signing renewal franchise agreement if all renewal conditions are met.

hiddenCosts.name: Site Design Fee / Kitchen Layout Design Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $1,200 for a preliminary site layout (PSL) or new kitchen layout (PKL); $750 for a remodel PKL; optional but commonly used.

hiddenCosts.name: Marketing Start-Up Fee (re-opening/remodel)
hiddenCosts.description: Minimum $10,000 required for re-opening or remodel events, not just initial opening.

operationalRisks.title: No Exclusive Territory
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees receive no exclusive or protected territory under the Franchise Agreement; franchisor and other franchisees may compete directly.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Remodel Obligations
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must refurbish and remodel at franchisor-specified dates at their own expense, which can be substantial and unpredictable.

operationalRisks.title: Royalties and Fees Owed Even If Losing Money
operationalRisks.description: CFF (5.9%) and CAF (5.0%) are due weekly on gross sales regardless of profitability.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Can Change System Standards Unilaterally
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor may modify manuals, standards, and business model without franchisee consent, potentially requiring additional investment.

operationalRisks.title: Restricted Supplier Sourcing (>95% of Purchases)
operationalRisks.description: More than 95% of purchases must be made from franchisor-designated or approved suppliers, which may be more expensive than open-market alternatives.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by mediation, arbitration, and/or litigation in the state where the restaurant is located, which may be costly and less favorable.

operationalRisks.title: Non-Compete Covenant Post-Termination
operationalRisks.description: For 24 months after expiration, termination, or transfer, franchisee and owners may not have any interest in a Competitive Business within 5 miles of the premises or any other Dunkin' restaurant.

operationalRisks.title: Pending Litigation – Franchisee Termination Disputes
operationalRisks.description: Multiple active lawsuits involving franchisee terminations (Minda's Donuts, Shree Krishna Donuts) and international franchise disputes (Phoenix Food Pakistan) create legal uncertainty.

operationalRisks.title: Technology Upgrade Obligations with No Cost Cap
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must upgrade the Restaurant Technology System at their sole cost with no contractual limit on frequency or cost.

operationalRisks.title: Loyalty Program Contribution Required
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must contribute 1.4% of Loyalty Program Sales (2025-2026) to the Loyalty Fund in addition to the 5.9% CFF and 5.0% CAF.

operationalRisks.title: Securitization Financing Structure
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor operates under a complex securitization financing structure; management services are performed by Dunkin' Brands under a management agreement, adding a layer of operational dependency.

rentDetail.source: Item 7, p. 49 — rent is described as variable and individually negotiated; no specific per-sqft rate is disclosed in Item 7. Building costs estimated at $83–$566/sqft for freestanding (750–3,100 sqft) and $59–$378/sqft for shopping center (600–2,800 sqft).

riskReasons: Capital gap of $932,450 is ~3.7x your liquid — meaningful outside financing required.
3 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $1,182,450 mid-point build-out, there is a $932,450 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $89,266/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: Dunkin' is a quick-service restaurant chain focused on coffee, donuts, and breakfast items sold through counter and drive-thru service.

benchmark.label: QSR / fast-casual
benchmark.dominantRisk: Prime cost and throughput per labor hour. Smaller footprint helps fixed costs, but COGS + labor still set the ceiling.
considerations: Budget COGS ~28–33% and labor ~22–28%; target prime cost under ~60%.
Verify average ticket and daily transaction counts with Item 20 operators, not the franchisor.
Watch for required remodels and equipment refresh cycles buried in Item 6/Item 11.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–18% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 7.3–9.2 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “QSR / fast-casual” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 6–10% category band.
Estimated at the 28–33% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 22–28% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor flagged its own financial condition as a risk. The figures we extracted look comparatively stable, so confirm directly against the most recent statements.
body: What we found (2025-12-28, audited by KPMG LLP): Net income of $732.91M on $1.40B of revenue — roughly 0.5x spent per $1 earned. Positive net worth of $1.33B. Cash of $3.00M against $54.45M of obligations due within a year (a current ratio of 2.16). Note: about 81% of those liabilities is deferred revenue — prepaid franchise fees the company owes as future services, not borrowed money. Netting it out, liabilities are still about 0.0x assets.

Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so Inspire Brands, Inc. is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
